is a [township municipality](/wiki/Township_municipality_(Quebec) "Township municipality (Quebec)") in south-west [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"). The population as of the [2021 Canadian census](/wiki/2021_Canadian_census "2021 Canadian census") was 1,995. The township completely surrounds the [Village of Hemmingford](/wiki/Hemmingford,_Quebec_(village) "Hemmingford, Quebec (village)"). The two entities (village and township) are locally referred to collectively as Hemmingford. The two share many things, such as the cost of the volunteer fire department, and both hold their councils and offices in the same building in the village.

([45°6′35.9994″N 73°34′18.696″W﻿ / ﻿45.109999833°N 73.57186000°W﻿ / 45.109999833; -73.57186000﻿ (Barrington, Hemmingford, QC)](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&params=45_6_35.9994_N_73_34_18.696_W_type:city_region:CA-QC&title=Barrington%2C+Hemmingford%2C+QC)) – a [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)") situated at the junction of Rte 219 and Fisher Road.

([45°4′18.9984″N 73°31′46.9992″W﻿ / ﻿45.071944000°N 73.529722000°W﻿ / 45.071944000; -73.529722000﻿ (Hallerton, Hemmingford, QC)](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&params=45_4_18.9984_N_73_31_46.9992_W_type:city_region:CA-QC&title=Hallerton%2C+Hemmingford%2C+QC)) – a [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)") situated on Williams Road.

([45°2′42″N 73°41′12.9978″W﻿ / ﻿45.04500°N 73.686943833°W﻿ / 45.04500; -73.686943833﻿ (The Fort, Hemmingford, QC)](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&params=45_2_42_N_73_41_12.9978_W_type:city_region:CA-QC&title=The+Fort%2C+Hemmingford%2C+QC)) – a [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)") located along [Quebec Route 202](/wiki/Quebec_Route_202 "Quebec Route 202") in the west towards [Havelock](/wiki/Havelock,_Quebec "Havelock, Quebec")

**Hemmingford** is a [township municipality](/wiki/Township_municipality_(Quebec) "Township municipality (Quebec)") in south-west [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"). The population as of the [2021 Canadian census](/wiki/2021_Canadian_census "2021 Canadian census") was 1,995. The township completely surrounds the [Village of Hemmingford](/wiki/Hemmingford,_Quebec_(village) "Hemmingford, Quebec (village)"). The two entities (village and township) are locally referred to collectively as Hemmingford. The two share many things, such as the cost of the volunteer fire department, and both hold their councils and offices in the same building in the village.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The geographic township was established in 1799, and named after [Hemingford Abbots](/wiki/Hemingford_Abbots "Hemingford Abbots"), in Huntingdonshire, England. The Saint-Romain-de-Hemmingford mission was set up in 1838 (which became a parish in 1853), while the first Anglican church was built 1843.[[1]](#cite_note-toponymie-1)

On July 1, 1845, the Township Municipality of Hemmingford was founded, but on September 1, 1847, it merged with Hinchinbrooke, Godmanchester, Dundee, St. Anicet, and Russeltown, to form the Municipality of Beauharnois Numéro Deux. On July 1, 1855, this merger was undone and the Township Municipality of Hemmingford was reestablished.[[5]](#cite_note-maires-5)

Subsequently, the territory of the township was successively partitioned in 1857, 1863, and 1878 to form to the new municipalities of [Franklin](/wiki/Franklin,_Quebec "Franklin, Quebec"), [Havelock](/wiki/Havelock,_Quebec "Havelock, Quebec") and the [Village of Hemmingford](/wiki/Hemmingford,_Quebec_(village) "Hemmingford, Quebec (village)") respectively.[[1]](#cite_note-toponymie-1)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

The township of Hemmingford is located due south of [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal") in the [Jardins-de-Napierville](/wiki/Les_Jardins-de-Napierville_Regional_County_Municipality "Les Jardins-de-Napierville Regional County Municipality") in the [Montérégie](/wiki/Mont%C3%A9r%C3%A9gie "Montérégie") region, in the south-east corner of the former [Huntingdon County](/wiki/Huntingdon_County,_Quebec "Huntingdon County, Quebec"). It sits between what was the Seigniory of Beauharnois and Seigniory of Lacolle. Prior to its survey in 1792 and 1793, the Township was called "Waste lands". Since the mid 1800's Hemmingford has been known as being part of one of the main apple producing regions in Quebec.

The township borders the townships of [Havelock](/wiki/Havelock,_Quebec "Havelock, Quebec"), [St-Chrysostome](/wiki/St-Chrysostome,_Quebec "St-Chrysostome, Quebec"), [St-Clotilde](/wiki/Sainte-Clotilde,_Quebec "Sainte-Clotilde, Quebec"), [Sherrington](/wiki/Saint-Patrice-de-Sherrington,_Quebec "Saint-Patrice-de-Sherrington, Quebec"), and [St-Bernard-de-Lacolle](/wiki/Saint-Bernard-de-Lacolle,_Quebec "Saint-Bernard-de-Lacolle, Quebec"). Its southern border is the [Canada–United States border](/wiki/Canada%E2%80%93United_States_border "Canada–United States border") with [Clinton County](/wiki/Clinton_County,_New_York "Clinton County, New York"), in [New York State](/wiki/New_York_State "New York State").

### Hamlets

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Hamlets")]

The following locations reside within the municipality's boundaries:[[1]](#cite_note-toponymie-1)

* **[Barrington](/wiki/Barrington,_Quebec "Barrington, Quebec")** ([45°6′35.9994″N 73°34′18.696″W﻿ / ﻿45.109999833°N 73.57186000°W﻿ / 45.109999833; -73.57186000﻿ (Barrington, Hemmingford, QC)](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&params=45_6_35.9994_N_73_34_18.696_W_type:city_region:CA-QC&title=Barrington%2C+Hemmingford%2C+QC)) – a [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)") situated at the junction of Rte 219 and Fisher Road.
* **Hallerton** ([45°4′18.9984″N 73°31′46.9992″W﻿ / ﻿45.071944000°N 73.529722000°W﻿ / 45.071944000; -73.529722000﻿ (Hallerton, Hemmingford, QC)](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&params=45_4_18.9984_N_73_31_46.9992_W_type:city_region:CA-QC&title=Hallerton%2C+Hemmingford%2C+QC)) – a [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)") situated on Williams Road.
* **The Fort** ([45°2′42″N 73°41′12.9978″W﻿ / ﻿45.04500°N 73.686943833°W﻿ / 45.04500; -73.686943833﻿ (The Fort, Hemmingford, QC)](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&params=45_2_42_N_73_41_12.9978_W_type:city_region:CA-QC&title=The+Fort%2C+Hemmingford%2C+QC)) – a [hamlet](/wiki/Hamlet_(place) "Hamlet (place)") located along [Quebec Route 202](/wiki/Quebec_Route_202 "Quebec Route 202") in the west towards [Havelock](/wiki/Havelock,_Quebec "Havelock, Quebec")

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

In the [2021 Census of Population](/wiki/2021_Canadian_census "2021 Canadian census") conducted by [Statistics Canada](/wiki/Statistics_Canada "Statistics Canada"), Hemmingford had a population of 1,995 living in 821 of its 899 total private dwellings, a change of 5% from its 2016 population of 1,900. With a land area of 157.22 km2 (60.70 sq mi), it had a population density of 12.7/km2 (32.9/sq mi) in 2021.[[4]](#cite_note-sc21-4)

## Attractions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hemmingford,_Quebec_(township)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Attractions")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/aa/Cervus_canadensis%2C_Parc_Safari_%282%29.JPG/250px-Cervus_canadensis%2C_Parc_Safari_%282%29.JPG)](/wiki/File:Cervus_canadensis,_Parc_Safari_(2).JPG)

Parc Safari

Some of the local attractions include [Parc Safari](/wiki/Parc_Safari "Parc Safari") and Hemmingford Golf and Country Club. The town supports many commercial apple growers, as well as cideries and wineries which include; Cidrerie Du Minot, Domaine du Salamandre, le Chat Botté, Vignes des Bacchantes and Cidrerie C.E.Petch.
